entry.title: how can I have a dynamic text with dimension input control on analytic application designer ?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I want to have a dynamic title table or text according toa dimension input control on my storie page on analitycs application designer.

The problem is when I press on "add dynamic text" I can only choose filter and not my input control ?

So, how can I have a dynamic text with input control on analytics application designer? A little context on the problem, I want to add the text on a popup window. I already sorted out the popup window.

Thanks for your Help !

#SAC #AnalyticsApp

entry.title: Enhancing Business Efficiency with SAP Build: Work Zone, Apps, and Process Automation
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) provides a robust suite of tools to help organizations build, extend, and enhance their digital capabilities. Among these tools are SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Apps, and SAP Build Process Automation. Each tool serves a unique purpose, from creating digital workplaces to developing custom applications and automating processes. This blog introduces these tools, outlines their key features, and provides examples to help you determine when to use each.

Intended Audience

This blog is intended for SAP developers, IT professionals, business process owners, and decision-makers looking to optimize digital experiences, streamline workflows, and accelerate digital transformation. Whether you are a developer seeking low-code solutions, an IT manager focusing on automation, or a business leader aiming to enhance employee productivity, this blog will help you understand how SAP Build tools can drive value for your organization.

Understanding the SAP Build Umbrella

SAP Build is a unified suite within SAP BTP that encompasses SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Apps, and SAP Build Process Automation. Together, these tools empower both business users and developers to design intuitive user interfaces, create custom applications, and automate complex workflows, all without the need for extensive coding skills.

  • SAP Build Work Zone focuses on creating a personalized digital workspace, where employees can access all their tools and information in one place.
  • SAP Build Apps is a low-code/no-code platform that enables rapid development of custom applications.
  • SAP Build Process Automation allows users to automate repetitive tasks and streamline business processes efficiently.

SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Work Zone enables organizations to create a unified digital workspace that enhances employee experience by integrating multiple applications, data sources, and services into a single, customizable portal. It is ideal for companies aiming to improve employee productivity and engagement by providing a personalized and consolidated workspace.

  • Key Features:
  • Unified access to SAP and third-party apps
  • Customizable user interfaces with drag-and-drop functionality
  • Pre-built templates for quick deployment
  • Role-based content delivery

Example Use Case: Create a central portal for HR that consolidates employee self-service tools, payroll information, training resources, and internal communication platforms.

SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps is a low-code/no-code platform that allows users to rapidly develop and deploy custom applications. Designed for both business users and developers, it offers a visual development environment to build responsive applications that can connect to SAP and non-SAP data sources.

  • Key Features:
  • Low-code/no-code application development
  • Drag-and-drop interface for rapid prototyping
  • Pre-built connectors for easy integration with various data sources
  • Flexibility to customize app logic and design

Example Use Case: Develop a mobile application for field service agents to access real-time inventory data, log service requests, and update task statuses without needing extensive coding knowledge.

SAP Build Process Automation

SAP Build Process Automation allows organizations to automate repetitive, manual tasks and streamline business processes using SAP’s AI and machine learning capabilities. This tool is perfect for companies looking to reduce errors, improve efficiency, and lower operational costs.

  • Key Features:
  • Visual workflow design and automation
  • Integration with SAP and third-party systems
  • Pre-built bot templates and AI services
  • Monitoring and analytics for process optimization

Example Use Case: Automate the end-to-end invoice processing workflow, from data extraction to approval, reducing manual intervention and accelerating processing times.

Comparison of SAP Build Tools

 

Conclusion and Next Steps

SAP BTP offers a powerful set of tools — SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Apps, and SAP Build Process Automation — to help organizations enhance employee experiences, rapidly develop applications, and automate processes. Start by identifying key areas where these tools can provide the most value, and consider initiating a small pilot project to explore their potential benefits. In the next blog, we’ll discuss advanced integration techniques and best practices for maximizing your SAP BTP investments.

entry.title: Maximizing ROI: 4 benefits of a business data fabric with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
In our data-driven world, it may seem inevitable that some information will remain scattered across disparate systems. Even after significant progress in organizing your data, you may still wonder if there’s a more effective way to manage all your data and whether a smoother approach to data integration could give you a leg up on the competition.

Rest assured, the answer is a definitive yes.

Imagine having self-service access to reliable data with business context intact. This is precisely what a business data fabric provides. But there are multiple paths to implementing a business data fabric, and some are more cost-effective than others.

4 ways organizations achieve remarkable savings with a business data fabric

In my previous blog, I talked about the advantages of choosing SAP Datasphere as the foundation for a business data fabric over a DIY blend of applications. Here, I’ll take a look at the ROI you can expect from implementing a business data fabric with SAP Datasphere based on the GigaOm TCO analysis, and how it depends in large part on your implementation approach.

1. Streamlining data integration

Integrating all data sources, including applications, clouds, and on-premise servers, provides a more cohesive view of your data and streamlines your analytics. This results in easier and faster access to impactful business insights. According to the GigaOm TCO analysis, the cost of using SAP Datasphere to achieve this was 69% lower than the cost of developing and establishing a DIY data fabric.

2. Achieving cost advantages

GigaOm found that companies saved up to 138% in total cost of ownership over three years when they use SAP Datasphere compared to DIY implementations. These are savings you can reinvest in other critical areas.

3. Increasing operational efficiency

In addition to saving money, you can cut your initial data fabric build timelines by more than half. GigaOm found that SAP Datasphere reduces the initial build timeline by about 2.5 times compared to DIY implementations, getting you up and running much faster and with less effort. And you won’t need to dedicate as much headcount to the build/migrate, CI/CD, and administration stages of implementation with SAP Datasphere, enabling you to achieve more with less.

4. Enabling scalability for future growth

Every business data fabric has its benefits, but it’s only as useful as it is scalable. The ideal solution allows your business and data solutions to grow at the same pace. SAP Datasphere is designed to support scalable growth. GigaOm found that the three-year TCO for a large data fabric implementation is $8.55 million with SAP Datasphere compared to $20.76 million with a DIY blend of applications. These savings provide long-term value and enables you to prepare your business for future challenges and opportunities.

Get started

SAP Datasphere is the foundation for a comprehensive, integrated business data fabric that streamlines the entire data management process. Visit the business data fabric page to learn more.

entry.title: SAP ECC 6 EHP 7 FInance Closing period Restriction setup and configuration
entry.content: During Month End Closing, need to allow Finance Users to make adjustments that impact FI records, but restrict the rest of the users to update anything that impact the FI records. How can I do it ?

entry.title: Re: Central Payment - CFIN Central Finance
entry.content:
Hi 
and Everyone

I saved the customizing table , I need to change the Central company code which is by default Grey field system automatically taken source company code as central company code as well.

For example : Source company code    |   Central company code 
                         AZN                                          AZN

Requirement : Source company code    |   Central company code 
                         AZN                                          ABCD

How to achieve this ? Please Advice 

 


Thanks,

entry.title: Re: ICMR workflow Adjustment and Closing & communication...
entry.content:
Hi @Annafri,

Usually, the inbox should receive the notification/approval task immediately when email was sent. If it's not the case, you can create a ticket to component BC-BMT-WFM, this is not an ICMR issue.

Regarding the issue "check not performed" in Manage Teams and Responsibilities app, it can't be reproduced on my end either. If it prevents you from enabling the workflow/team assignment, you can raise the issue to component CA-GTF-RM.

Thanks!

Grace

entry.title: Top 7 Ways Clean Core in SAP S/4HANA and RISE Will Save You Millions
entry.content:
In today’s business environment, CIOs face the challenge of driving innovation, controlling costs, and scaling efficiently. RISE with SAP and Clean Core are powerful solutions that help businesses modernize their ERP systems while saving millions. Below are the Top 7 ways these strategies can transform your organization.

1. Reduce Technical Debt and Complexity

The Problem: Over-customization of ERP systems often leads to technical debt, making future upgrades costly and complex.

The Solution: A Clean Core minimizes customizations, ensuring that SAP S/4HANA remains streamlined and easier to maintain. RISE with SAP further supports this with cloud infrastructure that reduces technical overhead.

Case Study: Versuni, formerly Philips Domestic Appliances, redesigned its entire IT landscape with RISE with SAP, completing a transformation that involved over 3,500 redesigned processes and 55 country operations in just 18 months. This change improved Versuni’s ability to innovate and stay agile in a digital-first world.

Read more about Versuni’s digital transformation.

2. Cost Predictability with RISE

OpEx vs. CapEx: With RISE with SAP, businesses can shift costs from capital expenditure (CapEx) to operating expenditure (OpEx), making costs more predictable and manageable.

Case Study: Nestlé utilized RISE with SAP to centralize its IT infrastructure, resulting in 99.97% system availability and the shutdown of nine data centers. By leveraging the cloud, Nestlé simplified its IT operations and enhanced financial predictability.

Explore Nestlé's global cloud transformation.

3. Faster, Seamless Upgrades

Continuous Innovation: RISE with SAP ensures businesses always run the latest version of SAP S/4HANA, allowing for seamless upgrades and continuous innovation without disrupting daily operations. Clean Core makes upgrades faster by reducing customization complexity.

Case Study: Mahindra & Mahindra improved developer efficiency by 35% and reduced back-order processing times by 20% after migrating to RISE with SAP. This helped the company stay competitive and agile in a fast-changing market.

Learn more about Mahindra’s transformation journey.

4. Lower Maintenance and Operational Costs

Reduced Support: A Clean Core reduces the need for frequent maintenance, as the simplified system architecture lowers the overall operational costs. RISE with SAP provides a managed cloud solution that further decreases IT infrastructure costs.

Case Study: Inchcape, the world’s leading automotive distributor, used RISE with SAP to modernize its IT infrastructure. The result? 100% system availability and a 40-50% increase in communication speed across the organization, making Inchcape more responsive and adaptable to new business models.

Watch Inchcape’s journey with RISE.

5. Innovation Without Disruption

SAP BTP for Extensibility: The SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) allows companies to build side-by-side extensions without disrupting the core ERP system. This flexibility ensures continuous innovation while maintaining system stability.

Case Study: Delta Light, a leading architectural lighting company, implemented RISE with SAP to standardize global processes, achieve nearly 100% data accuracy, and reduce total cost of ownership by 20% over five years.

Read more about Delta Light’s process standardization.

6. Long-Term Scalability and Future-Readiness

AI and Machine Learning: With RISE with SAP, businesses can easily integrate technologies like AI and machine learning, ensuring long-term scalability and future-proofing operations.

Case Study: Salzgitter AG, a European steel manufacturer, used RISE with SAP to drive decarbonization and streamline operations across 150 subsidiaries. This setup provides a strong foundation for future innovations and sustainability.

Discover how Salzgitter is leading in sustainable steelmaking.

7. Sustainability and Compliance Benefits

Sustainability Tracking: RISE with SAP helps companies meet sustainability goals by improving transparency in supply chains and tracking environmental impact, ensuring regulatory compliance.

Case Study: ElectraMeccanica Vehicles Corp., an electric vehicle startup, used RISE with SAP to streamline operations, improve sustainability, and eliminate 100% of manual processes. The company achieved zero downtime and completed its migration in just eight months.

Learn more about ElectraMeccanica’s journey.

Conclusion: Achieve Agility and Cost Savings with RISE and Clean Core

By adopting RISE with SAP and the Clean Core strategy, your organization can reduce costs, simplify processes, and ensure long-term scalability. These seven simple strategies not only save millions but also position your organization to thrive in a rapidly evolving market.

Take Action: Start your journey with RISE and Clean Core to ensure your organization’s efficiency, scalability, and sustainable growth.

What is RISE with SAP?

RISE with SAP is a managed cloud offering that enables a smooth, secure migration of on-premise ERP systems – including SAP ERP and SAP S/4HANA – to the cloud. With embedded intelligence, real-time insights, and advanced technologies, businesses can improve compliance and build resilience for the future.

To learn more about RISE with SAP, visit this page.

Ready to start your Cloud ERP migration journey?

Visit here to explore how RISE with SAP can accelerate your business transformation.

entry.title: Issue with Range Partition (/B**/FKO**)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We have recently received an alert for the table SAPB***./B**/FKO** , specifically Partition 4, in our BPC Production system (***). Upon investigation, we found that the Range partition was executed on the key "KEY_KOII***" with the following ranges: 0, 1, 2, and others (*). However, only data for range 0 is present while ranges 1 and 2 are missing.

Table Name: /B**/FKO**

It seems that the data has been recorded under partition ID 4 (i.e., *). Looks like the partitioning was not performed correctly (in DIGITS), and the ranges were not properly assigned during the process.

Could you please provide a solution to resolve this partitioning issue? Is there an alternative solution that can be performed online without requiring an outage? Please let us know if you need any additional information. I've attached screenshots of the table for reference.

Thanks,
***


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Range Partition (/B**/FKO**)
entry.content:
The issue you are encounterring that is a typical problem, what you need to do for handling this situation is following this note:

- note
2019973 - Handling Very Large Data Volumes in SAP BW on SAP HANA
2374652 - Handling Very Large Data Volumes in SAP BW/4HANA
-> Handling Large Data Volumes - 2 Billion Limit Value Reached

You need apply script TABLE_PLACEMENT.zip and perform reorg after that.

entry.title: Re: Chinese Localisation- The period close account selection criteria does not include the revenue a
entry.content: Hi All, Is there any update or solution to this?

entry.title: BW on HANA Migration to BW4 PCE with mixed modeling
entry.content:
HI All,

We have one question for BW4 PCE migration. We use external HANA view and BW modeling in BW on HANA currently. As what we known it is mixed hana  modeling.

Is it possible mirgrate to BW PCE migration, and keeping the mixed modeling way in the future.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Renumbering of Chart of Account GL in SAP S/4 Hana Public Cloud
entry.content: Hi Experts, Can I ask a question related to this topic: In case we use our own G/L account numbers (i.e., we renumber the standard G/L accounts and create the new G/L accounts), will the updates that occur twice a year (in February and August) have any impacts on our own G/L accounts?

entry.title: Re: SAP Certifications and UID...all you need to know!
entry.content: how to create P-user ID or S-user ID.

entry.title: Re: Screen Personas : Change Cell Value In Table
entry.content:
I don't know what transaction your screen is from, but I tried changing the 'Customer Material Number' cell value in the first row of the item overview table in VA02's 'Sales' tab and this worked fine for me:

session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/tabsTAXI_TABSTRIP_OVERVIEW/tabpT\\01/ssubSUBSCREEN_BODY:SAPMV45A:4400/subSUBSCREEN_TC:SAPMV45A:4900/tblSAPMV45ATCTRL_U_ERF_AUFTRAG").modifyCell(0, 'VBAP-KDMAT', 'test');

The value was changed to test as expected. Of course, the table and field must be editable for this to work.

entry.title: RE: How to regulate CALM collected data
entry.content:
We are currently working with the CALM integrations for health monitoring and CTMS, as CALM is providing 8GB of data we would like to regulate the CALM collected data. And we would like to optimize our data management practices, we need guidance on how to regulate and manage the CALM collected data effectively.

Could you please provide us with detailed information on the following:

What procedures or tools does SAP provide for archiving old data or deleting data that is no longer needed? Can you provide guidelines for implementing these processes effectively?

How can we segment or filter CALM data to focus on specific metrics or operational areas? Are there any built-in features or recommended methods for customizing data views?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: RE: How to regulate CALM collected data
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

As documented in the guide Fire-Use, you can monitor the SAP HANA memory consumption in SAP Cloud ALM in the Health Monitoring app by selecting your SAP Cloud ALM service and checking the SAP Cloud ALM HANA Memory metric.
You can see the details of the memory usage for different function areas.

To reduce your memory usage, you can change your housekeeping settings in SAP Cloud ALM for operations apps. For more information, see Housekeeping on SAP Support Portal.

To cover extended use scenarios, you can subscribe to additional services.

Best regards,
Peng

entry.title: Re: How to install more than one DP Agent on a machine (W...
entry.content: I assume you use the different directory for installation , for the port configuration, it is in dpagentconfig.ini file under dpagent installation folder.

entry.title: Re: How to install more than one DP Agent on a machine (W...
entry.content: framework.adminport= framework.listenerPort

entry.title: Re: Date format validation in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Balu,

 

could you please let us know what exactly you are trying to achieve?

Are you trying to bring in data to datasphere via a replication flow? where do you want to validate the date?

entry.title: Call RAP methods from Control.js
entry.content:
Hello ,

I have an application developed using restful abap where I have few address fields on the object page.

My requirement is to have custom action and by selecting, it should validate the address and give the user a popup with suggested or corrected address. 

Since its not possible to have a popup with RAP itself, I created this custom action button using the guided development in SAP BAS. That created function call which is located in controller.js.

Question is: is there a way I can call my RAP method from controller.js to validate the address when user clicks on the button? 

Appreciate your response

entry.title: Re: change to Chart of Accounts Template
entry.content:
Hi,

Chart of Account template is an initialization step for working. If the same is selected and entries have been posted, then change to the same is not possible. Even if the transactions posted are reversed, it will not matter. The change is not possible.

You can check, Financial Report Templates to see if you can create your own customized template for B/S, P&L or Trial Balance. Not sure if that is helpful.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: How to create RAP Action to generate a file to download?
entry.content: Same Requirement

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I tried to run the abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022 via Docker in my Windows 10 local machine but I got an error message FAIL: process hdbindexserver HDB Indexserver-HDB not running

Basic info and background:

I am able to run the ABAPTRIAL 2022 in my Linux machine (Fedora).

My host machine has 64 GB of RAM and there are 237 GB of disk space available in drive: C.

My docker desktop is running with WSL 2 based engine.

I already have the ABAP 1909 trial docker image and the 1909 container works fine (as shown below).

I am quite sure that the system resources of my machine have passed the minimum requirements to run it.

The command I executed was

 

docker run --stop-timeout 3600 -it -p 3200:3200 -p 3300:3300 -p 3601:3601 -p 8443:8443 -p 30213:30213 -p 50000:50000 -p 50001:50001 --name a4h2022 -h vhcala4hci sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022 -skip-limits-check -agree-to-sap-license

 

note: I named it as a4h2022 because a4h is already occupied by the 1909. I also though if this is the root cause of this error but it seems it wasn't not because I already tried to rename the 1909 container to another name and run the 2022 with --name a4h but I still got the same result.

This is some system information inside the container.

Has anyone encountered this kind of problem? Any suggestion like which log file that I should take a look into.

I already tried to google it but still could not find the solution. Any suggestion you can provide would be greatly appreciated.

Regards,

Wises K.

entry.title: Re: SAP C4C free trial tennant for study
entry.content: tks. I opend it.

entry.title: Re: EPM 刷新出现错误
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Please follow the solution in KBA 2878435 - Error : "SY_530", "UJ0_EXCEPTION_005" and see if the issue can be resolved.

For your future reference, for EPM Add-in issues, please choose the SAP managed tag SAP Business Planning and Consolidation, version for SAP NetWeaver and EPM Add-In for Excel so your question can be directed to the right experts.

Best regards,
Amy

entry.title: Re: SAC Planning customize email text
entry.content: I have created an idea for this requirement - please vote https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/330713/?section=sectionDetails

entry.title: Re: ABAP Calling last calling program in the stack
entry.content:
@ulrich_mhrke

This is screen flow logic:

PROCESS BEFORE OUTPUT.
  MODULE status_0400.

PROCESS AFTER INPUT.
  MODULE user_command_0400.


PROCESS ON VALUE-REQUEST.

And for Module user_command_400.

 CASE sy-ucomm.
    WHEN 'NEWMAT'.
      "test run
      SET PARAMETER ID 'OPTION' FIELD 'MAT'.
      CALL TRANSACTION 'ZTRANS'.

 NEWMAT is function key declared for the button on screen 400.

entry.title: Re: Ledger 0L data are there any duplicates for the example 3 in the note 2554992 ?
entry.content: Or, does the depreciation area 15 not post to general ledger？It should be set as '0-Area Does Not Post' with the G/L indicator in the table view V_T093_00N.

entry.title: Re: How to create RAP Action to generate a file to download?
entry.content: Utilizing BTP/BAS, create a UI5 enhancement project for the RAP application and enhance a Custom SAP UI5 application with XSLX library by SheetJS to download as Excel File for a custom view table.

entry.title: Re: Fiori elements list report: Aggregate functions are not allowed in transactional views?
entry.content: Check Support.sap.com for relevant notes such as 2489473 - ABAP CDS: Aggregate functions in View Extends

entry.title: Re: Extract Document details using Document Information Extraction service with ABAP
entry.content: Excellent Blog and it really worked for me. Thanks a lot JoseMunoz.

entry.title: SAP Central Business Configuration Alternate
entry.content:
Dear All,

Can anyone confirm if it is mandatory to configure SAP Cloud (Public) / Grow systems only using the SAP Central Business Configuration?

Is there an alternative way to configure Public Cloud systems without using SAP Central Business Configuration? If yes, please share the details.

Additionally, is there any license requirement or prerequisite for accessing SAP Central Business Configuration?

I have a demo system for SAP Public Cloud. Is SAP Central Business Configuration available for demo systems?

entry.title: Re: SAP FICO Handling of Expenses Before Sales
entry.content:
Hi

You might want to check this functionality and adopt it to your needs:

Manage Condition Contracts - Sales Rebates | SAP Help Portal

Thanks & Best Regards

Sanil Bhandari

entry.title: Re: SAP Service Cloud - Version 2 - Utilities add on - Ho...
entry.content:
Hello,

Premises cannot be created in Service Cloud v2, they should be created in your backend and replicated to Service Cloud.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Help needed in HR forms WT1000 (Working hours) cumula...
entry.content: Then in that case Check Processing Class 20  the wage type should have without split and let me know the output

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

Hello @David_Chaviano 

My name is Dat Nguyen and I am 30 years old.

I identify myself as: People looking to switch their careers mid-life.

I have just finished the learning journey "Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation". In the future, I want to boost my career with Production planning skills.

 

Regards,

Dat Nguyen

entry.title: Re: Why FM HR_ECM_DELETE_INFOTYPE does not work?
entry.content:
Thank you @ulrich_mhrke .

That worked perfectly fine!

Ans: call the function module HR_ECM_FLUSH_INFOTYPE after FM HR_ECM_DELETE_INFOTYPE.

entry.title: Special GL
entry.content:
Hi SAP Gurus,

I have a requirement where I need to create a Special GL for customer retention so that the retention amount would be on hold in this special GL until the receivable date is due.

 

Can anyone please help me with this as how to move forward with this.

entry.title: create service key for SAP Cloud ALM.
entry.content:
We had Cloud ALM provisioned recently and we need a service key to be generated for the service.

kindly do the needful


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: create service key for SAP Cloud ALM.
entry.content:
Refer to the following documentation to create the SAP Cloud ALM API instance and generate the service key.

0003121402 - How to Enable SAP Cloud ALM API
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/enabling-sap-cloud-alm-api-in-cloud-foundry

entry.title: Re: A new generation of CDS views: CDS view entities
entry.content:
I understand that VIEW ENTITIES are the recommended approach. However, I recently encountered a scenario where VIEW ENTITIES do not function as expected when accessed in the HANA database. While there are no syntax errors, they fail to return any data. To resolve the issue, I had to create a classical CDS view, which works both in ABAP reports and the HANA database.

Could you please provide any insights or suggestions on how to access VIEW ENTITIES directly in the HANA database?

Regards,
Yellappa.

entry.title: Re: In a asset class, is it possible to assign different...
entry.content:
Hi @VENKATARAMAKRISHNA1 

it would be great if you could specify the system and version you are using: ECC or S/4HANA (New Asset Accounting) or S/4HANA with UPA (Intelligent Asset Accounting)

Ideally you should be able to use AS02 if you are using ECC to update the Depreciation key. However, please note that once your depreciation key is changed, the depreciation for open fiscal year will be recalculated.

Thanks & Best Regards

Sanil Bhandari

entry.title: Re: The UploadSet control in SAPUI5 cannot upload files properly
Re: Questions re: "ABAP Core Data Services" developer challenge
entry.content:
Hi @jonwalnikhilesh 

You should be receiving the badges soon. We are in the process.

entry.title: Re: The UploadSet control in SAPUI5 cannot upload files properly
entry.content: Please refer to this note to solve the issue: 3377504

entry.title: Different E Mail Address for Bill To and Ship To Party
entry.content:
We have a requirement where we want to send a sales order confirmation to a separate e-mail ID and send billing document to a separate ID of the same sales order and same Bill To and Ship To partners. Please let us know how we can achieve this. Output Parameter Determination | SAP Help Portal


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Different E Mail Address for Bill To and Ship To Party
entry.content:
Dear customer,

This issue falls into consulting category that is out of the scope of standard Support.

Could you please take a look at the following KBA?

2706322 - What is Support – What is Consulting: Cloud Solutions

Regarding your requirement, please go through the following documentations for more insights.

  1. support.sap.com/content/dam/SAAP/Sol_Pack/Library/Setup/1LQ_Set-Up_EN_XX.pdf
  2. Output Parameter Determination | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Issue with Range Partition (/B**/FKO**)
entry.content:
Hi

Sub-partition 1 and Sub-partition 4 in InfoCube refers to Compressed requests and UnCompressed requests.

Infocubes are partitioned ROUDROBIN as first level and Range as second level based on package ID and you can not change it.

So two possible solutions;

1. Compress your Infocube so that uncompress data will move from Sub-partition 4 and Sub-partition 1 to allow more room for data in Sub-partition 4

OR

2, Perform partitoning at DB level to change first level ROUNDROBIN partitions based on your table placement setting. See SAP Note 2069235 - How to Change the Initial Number of First-level Partitions for BW generated Tables in SAP HANA.

Regards

Tanuj

Hii

I have created a custom field in Purchase requisition item level in public cloud . I have three values in the dropdown , namely HIGH ,MEDIUM and LOW . I want the system to display the values HIGH , MEDIUM and LOW in the custom field based on the material group of that line item.

Thanks

Shraddha

entry.title: Re: Refurbishment Process in Plant Maintenance
entry.content: Does this Process works for QM managed material. For a material with QM managed with NEW batch procurement happens and stock gets updated later it will be issued after a while same material will be subjected to refurb process during GR it creates inspection lot and inspection stock tab missing. After completion of UD del qty in Work order its not getting updated..is there any note which specifies it wont support for QM process.

entry.title: Re: Deleting data deleted in the source system with idoc...
entry.content:
The easiest way is to use IDOCs with Change Pointers. They will create in each segment MSGFN that indicates what happened:

In S/4 HANA concept would be to enable Events - you will get a notification what happens (e.g. create/delete/update) into your Event Infrastructure while you have to subscribe to that event from Cloud Integration and call back the API to read the relevant payload that you need to send to the receiver.

IMO  - the Eventing is not yet major enough and I would still wait a bit before using this technology - but you cannot make something wrong if you setup change pointers. 

BR Helmut

entry.title: Re: Using FMCYCOPI_CO to copy planning in ACDOCP to Budget in FM only 2 dimensions are transferred
entry.content: Please, did anybody use FMCYCOPY_CO Tcode to copy CO planning entries (COSP/ACDOCP) to budget in BCS but using three dimensions?

entry.title: Re: Launch ByDesign UIs from External Applications
entry.content:
Hi Knut,

Thank you for creating this helpful document. 
Recently we are referring to it and we would like to raise a question about purchase order.

We noticed that in the document, you raised an example of purchase order, which is only by UUID instead of both Purchase Order ID and UUID. So we tested in our system and as a result, UUID worked and PO ID didn't work.

We would like to confirm if purchase order can only be visited by UUID.

Meanwhile, we went to Test Shell to check how system stored the documents. For purchase order, it is stored as node ID only but for sales order, it is stored as Sales Order ID.  We wonder if this is the reason why only UUID can be used to access purchase orders.

Looking forward to your reply.

Best Regards,
Jiuying

entry.title: Implement the compar feature in UI5
entry.content:
Hello,
I am interested in developing a viewport in ui5.
I would like to apply the [visual compare] function in SAP VEV to UI5 viewport.

First of all, I know that ui5 is using the viewport threejs library.
I understand that there is a way to compare two models using the [Three-bvh-csg] library in threejs.
Attached is the link below:
https://discourse.threejs.org/t/three-bvh-csg-a-library-for-performing-fast-csg-operations/42713

Additionally, the VEV attaches the [visual compare] function as an image.
It would be nice if you could give me a hint or help on how I can combine the gray and blue models in the image.

entry.title: EarlyWatch AlertのSAP バックボーンへの送信について
entry.content:
ご担当者様

お世話になっております。

現在、EWAの設定を行いまして「SD1 のEarlyWatch AlertのSAP バックボーンへの送信」で黄色信号の警告が出ております。SD1はS/4開発機のSIDですが、このシステムをSAPへ接続する必要があるという警告で認識あっていますでしょうか？

また、Solution ManagerシステムはSAPへバックボーン通信が設定済みなのですが、Solution Manager経由で各システムの情報を転送することは可能でしょうか？

以上、ご回答よろしくお願いいたします。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: EarlyWatch AlertのSAP バックボーンへの送信について
entry.content:
ご担当者様

平素よりお世話になっております。
お問い合わせにつき、下記回答させていただきます。

ご質問：現在、EWAの設定を行いまして「SD1 のEarlyWatch AlertのSAP バックボーンへの送信」で黄色信号の警告が出ております。SD1はS/4開発機のSIDですが、このシステムをSAPへ接続する必要があるという警告で認識あっていますでしょうか？
＝＞開発システムのEWAはSAP側に送信できません。この部分の黄色信号の警告を無視すれば問題ないです。

詳細につき、弊社KBA3388673の「resolution」部分をご確認ください。
KBA3388673ーHow to deal with the yellow alert assigned to check "Ready for 2020" in SAP EarlyWatch Alert report

ご質問：また、Solution ManagerシステムはSAPへバックボーン通信が設定済みなのですが、Solution Manager経由で各システムの情報を転送することは可能でしょうか？

＝＞EWAレポートをSAP側に送信することは、IT 管理者ロールが本稼動システム、品質保証システム、またはテストシステムの場合では設定することができます。開発システムの場合は設定できません。

詳細：1684537 - EarlyWatch Alert Not Sent to SAP: Troubleshooting Guide
ーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーー抜粋ーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーー
The column 'Send to SAP' option is only enabled for status Production System, Quality Assurance System or Test System.
ーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーー

以上です。宜しくお願い致します。
SAPサポート
ショウセイ

Prasanth

entry.title: Re: In a asset class, is it possible to assign different...

entry.title: How to import CIM model for setup synchronization between SLD and LMDB
entry.content:
In Solman configuration we are in solman Synchronization phase in infrastructure preparation, we are getting below error "No CIM model version found; import at least 1.6.59 ".

Please guide us how to import CIM model.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to import CIM model for setup synchronization bet...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Please refer to below steps for downloading and importing CIM Model to SLD, more details could be found in Note669669.

If using the SLD: In "Administration -> Details -> Data", you will find the current version of the CIM model and SAP CR Content (for example, 1.6.54 for the CIM model and SAP_CR 14.1 for SAP CR Content).

Initial import of CIM model:

If no SAP CR Content exists in your system, you must fill it first. Initial versions of the CIM model and SAP CR Content are delivered with the system installation, and can be directly imported. To reduce the number of required imports, you can procure the current CIM model version and an initial full import for the current SAP CR Content version from the SAP Support Portal as an alternative. This full import is used only for initial filling, and can be imported only to a namespace that does not yet contain SAP CR Content. The full import can be used, for example, to compile a reference namespace for a repair of SAP CR Content (SAP Note 1093168). For a full import, the latest available version of the CIM model is required.

The initial full import is available in the SAP Support Portal:

https://me.sap.com/softwarecenter

Under "Installations & Upgrades" (link: Access downloads) -> By Alphabetical Index (A-Z) -> Letter C -> "SAP CR CONTENT". Select the "SAP CR Content YYYY" entry with the highest available year and download the file offered in the "Installation" section.

For the initial filling, you must therefore import two files in this sequence:

  1. The current CIM model "cimsap....zip" (see the "Update of SAP CR Content" section below)

Importing the updates into an SLD:

First, import the latest available model (cimsap....zip) by choosing "SLD UI -> Administration -> Import". You cannot ignore any warning that may occur telling you that a newer model has already been loaded. You must therefore choose the "Cancel Import" button, and not "Import Anyway". An old model is not allowed to be loaded over a later model. You can import the same model version and this does not lead to any warning. For more information, see SAP Note 1045423.

Best regards,

Leo

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How to group employees based on their hireDate attribute via Identity Provisioning?
entry.content:
Hi,

Wondering if anyone can help determine why I cant get hireDate from SuccessFactors into Local Identity Dir.

I've added the following to the properties of my source SuccessFactors transformation in IPS

sf.user.attributes = userId,username,status,email,lastName,firstName,lastModifiedDateTime,personKeyNav,hireDate,terminationDate

along with code 

{
"sourcePath": "$.hireDate",
"targetPath": "$.hireDate",
"optional": true,
"functions": [
{
"function": "manipulateDate",
"sourceDateFormat": "Date(milliseconds)",
"targetDateFormat": "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS"
}
]
},

in local Identity dir target system i have the following transformation code to assign hireDate to validFrom field

       {
                "sourcePath": "$.hireDate",
                "targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User']['validFrom']",
                "optional": true
            },

But no luck - I cant seem to see hireDate at all.

Any ideas ?

Ta
Anita

entry.title: Re: SAP Central Business Configuration Alternate
entry.content:
Hi,

the SAP Central Business Configuration does not need a license on its own. You just have to have the appropriate licenses for the SAP solutions you are configuring.

Best regards,

Torben

entry.title: Re: OData missing after publishing Custom analytical quer...
entry.content:
You have to activate the OData Service in the transaction

/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE

https://help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_nw75/7.5.5/en-US/1b/023c1cad774eeb8b85b25c86d94f87/frameset.htm

entry.title: Two-tier ERP and Group Reporting: Deployment Options with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Potential Two-tier ERP Customers running their operational ERP systems on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition or SAP S/4HANA on-premise can connect to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition to perform group reporting in different ways subjected to business requirements. Below three are most used type of deployments 

  1. Connect SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition subsidiaries with Group Reporting Data Collection (GRDC)
  2. Run Group Reporting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition connecting to private cloud ERPs
  3. Connect SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition subsidiaries with Central Finance

 

1.Connect SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition subsidiaries with GRDC -  Leverage Group Reporting & integration with the set up at Headquarters running on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition when subsidiaries are running on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition

Key capabilities with this type of deployment includes

  • Subsidiaries can run on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition
  • Financial statements & trail balances can be uploaded to GRDC at Headquarters.
  • Mostly Group Financial Consolidation is performed at Headquarters running on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition.

     

Note : 

 *     GRDC can be deployed both on S/4HANA Public Cloud or Private Cloud Edition

 Data upload/replication can be manual or via SAP delivered Integration

 

2. Run Group Reporting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition connecting to private cloud ERPs - Group Profitability can be measured in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition which is typically performing Group Reporting on behalf of Headquarters when subsidiary is running on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition

Key capabilities with this type of deployment includes

  • Running Group Reporting in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition that leverages latest Group Reporting innovations delivered through continuous upgrades
  • Financial statements & trail balances can be uploaded to GRDC 
  • Any ERPs that cannot (yet) transition to the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition can stay in private cloud

     

Note : 

 *     GRDC can be deployed both on S/4HANA Public Cloud or Private Cloud Edition

 Data upload/replication can be manual or via SAP delivered Integration

 

3.Connect SAP S/4HANA public cloud edition subsidiaries with Central Finance - Group Consolidation and profitability can be measured at Headquarters running on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition with Central Finance

Key capabilities with this type of deployment includes

  • Real-time accounting document replication to central finance system running on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition 
  • Subsidiaries can run on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition

     

Note: Customers can further evaluate each deployment option to select the one that best suits their business requirements and organizational needs.

entry.title: Re: SAP Central Business Configuration Alternate
entry.content: Thanks Tolschewski, can you please also confirm that is this the only possible option to configure the system or there is also some other alternate to configure the cloud system.

entry.title: RAP vs CAP (database-wise)
entry.content:
I’m seeking clarification on the pros and cons of using RAP and CAP strictly in terms of database usage. Based on my understanding, RAP utilizes the S/4HANA Cloud database within the ABAP environment, whereas CAP works with the S/4HANA Cloud database via the HANA DB Explorer.

In a recent internal discussion with SAP at our company, it was mentioned that the HANA database accessed through the HANA DB Explorer is more stable compared to the one used in the ABAP environment. This has caused some confusion, as I haven't been able to find clear information or articles to verify this.

Could you explain the difference between these databases?

Are there any advantages of one over the other?

Could you provide sample use cases where one would be preferred over the other?

Additionally, in terms of backup and restoration, is it possible to perform this for both environments?

entry.title: Re: Optimized Story Experience – SAP SuccessFactors Peopl...
entry.content:
Hi @Jeff 

it seems to be fixed for "Cross-tab" in Optimized Mode. Full Excel export is supported there. In terms of "Non-Aggregated list" behavior is same as before, only "point of view" (maximum 500 lines) Excel Export is supported.

Christian

entry.title: Re: same issuse i am facing will you find the solution pl...
entry.content:
Hi @tirumala 

As the error message indicates, please Enable Cross Context SSO and try again.

  1. Go into your app in Mobile Services and select the Security tab
  2. Click Edit for the Application Settings
  3. Check the Enable Cross Context SSO option
  4. Save
  5. Go back to the Editor and try again

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Story filter becomes open for user modification after being altered with script api
entry.content: I would see this as the expected behaviour, since the user is essentially adjusting the filter via the script. Admittedly it would be nice to extend the setFilter method to allow a non-user editable filter. This is particularly important for Planning scenarios where you need to ensure the user can’t inadvertently change the planning area. For our situation, I have turned off all navigation on the table so the user at runtime has no ability to manipulate the filters. In the case of a fileDataSource, this would mean hiding the filter option in the toolbar and ensuring the filter panel is set to hidden. Cheers, Ivan.

entry.title: Re: SAP Central Business Configuration Alternate
entry.content: I am not an expert on this, but I think you can also use SSCUIs. Maybe check out this blog entry: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-public-cloud-hints-and-tips-for-configuration-and-sscuis/ba-p/13368211
